data.title: Coinbase taps Kalshi to develop prediction markets platform
data.description:
Coinbase plans a prediction markets platform using Kalshi’s regulated system. Users can trade USDC or USD across sports, politics, and tech events. Move aligns with Coinbase’s goal to become an “everything exchange. ” Coinbase is preparing to enter the rapidly growing prediction markets sector, leveraging the regulated infrastructure of Kalshi to build its own platform
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/coinbase-taps-kalshi-to-develop-prediction-markets-platform/

data.title: Revolut Partners with Polygon for Stablecoin Transactions in Europe
data.description:
According to PANews, on November 18, European neobank Revolut announced a partnership with Polygon Labs, the Ethereum Layer 2 scaling network, to utilize Polygon as its preferred technology stack for stablecoin transfers, payments, and transactions. Customers in the UK and the European Economic Area (non-EU countries) can use the Revolut app and Polygon blockchain to remit cryptocurrencies such as USDC, USDT, and POL. This integration aligns with the EU's MiCA regulatory framework and considers the regulatory requirements for stablecoins like USDT.

The press release states that Revolut will enable Polygon network support for stablecoin transfers in its app by early December 2024. As of November this year, transactions processed through the Revolut app using Polygon have exceeded $690 million. The integration offers a seamless Web3 experience within the Revolut app, featuring core functionalities such as:

- Instant cross-border transfers of stablecoins USDC, USDT, and POL.

- Crypto card payments within the Revolut app, allowing users to pay for everyday expenses using stablecoins.

- Support for POL tokens, enabling users to stake or trade POL directly through the Revolut app, simplifying access to the Polygon ecosystem without external wallets.

- Direct fiat currency exchange within Revolut, lowering entry barriers and promoting stablecoin circulation, with over $3.6 billion in stablecoin supply on Polygon.

Revolut serves over 65 million users across 38 countries, including 14 million crypto users. Since 2017, the bank has offered cryptocurrency trading services in certain regions, but due to regulatory uncertainties, this feature was discontinued for U.S. users in 2023.

Last month, the company obtained a MiCA license in Cyprus, potentially paving the way for launching its own stablecoin. In June, insiders revealed that Revolut was considering launching its own stablecoin, following the expansion of its cryptocurrency services to over 30 countries with the introduction of Revolut X a year ago.

The integration with Polygon follows the network's significant payment upgrade, named Rio, which enhances speed, efficiency, and reliability, achieving nearly instant settlement of up to 5,000 transactions per second, ensuring high throughput and low latency.

In addition to Revolut, other institutions like Stripe, Mastercard, Flutterwave, and Reliance Jio have chosen Polygon as their cryptocurrency payment channel. Revolut's adoption of Polygon reflects a broader trend as businesses seek to reduce transaction costs and improve service efficiency. The long-term application of these systems will depend on their stability, compliance, and user acceptance of volatile digital assets.

data.title: OKX will suspend deposit and withdrawal services for ETH, X Layer, and other networks on December 4th to support Ethereum network upgrades.
data.description: According to the official announcement, to ensure the smooth progress of the Ethereum (ETH) network upgrade and hard fork, deposit and withdrawal services for Ethereum (ETH), Xlayer (OKB), Arbitrum (ARB), Optimism (OP), Base (BASE), Starknet (STRK), zkSync Era, Linea, Unichain, and other related tokens within the same network will be suspended on December 4, 2025 at 05:30 (UTC+8). After the upgrade is completed, OKX will reopen the deposit and withdrawal services for the aforementioned tokens once the network is stable, without further notice.

data.title: Binance Introduces New Yield Arena Offers with Enhanced APR
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has unveiled new offerings in its Yield Arena, providing users with opportunities to earn rewards through various financial products. These offerings include Simple Earn, ETH Staking, SOL Staking, and Dual Investment, each designed to cater to different investment preferences and risk appetites.

**Earn Spotlight Limited-Time Offers**

Eligible users holding at least 0.01 USDe for 24 hours can benefit from a 6% APR from 2025-11-13 00:00 (UTC) to 2025-11-20 23:59 (UTC). Additionally, users subscribing to XTZ Locked Products between 2025-11-13 10:00 (UTC) and 2026-02-11 23:59 (UTC) can enjoy up to 29% APR. Those holding BFUSD will receive enhanced annualized yield rewards, including a Base APR plus an additional 5% from 2025-11-03 04:30 (UTC) to 2025-12-01 23:59 (UTC).

**ETH & SOL Staking**

Subscribers to ETH and SOL staking until 2025-12-31 will enjoy higher APRs, with ETH Staking offering up to 2.5% APR and SOL Staking up to 5.6% APR. For on-chain yields, users subscribing to the Aave-Plasma USDT Locked Product can earn up to 4.5% APR in USDT starting from 2025-09-25 12:00 (UTC).

**Dual Investment**

Participants in Dual Investment and the November Monthly Leaderboard program can earn up to $5,888 in rewards between 2025-11-03 05:00 (UTC) and 2025-11-30 23:59 (UTC). Flexible products such as USDT, USDC, ETH, SOL, and RESOLV offer real-time APRs with bonus tiered APRs, while locked products like XTZ, MMT, and APE provide fixed-term APRs.

Binance reserves the right to adjust APRs without prior notice, and offers are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Users can redeem assets early in Simple Earn Locked Products, with the principal returned to their Spot Accounts. Rewards from ongoing Launchpools are automatically distributed to users holding BNB Flexible and Locked Products positions.

data.title: Binance Expands Soft Staking with Five New Tokens
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has expanded its Soft Staking service to include five additional tokens: Tron (TRX), Aptos (APT), IOTA (IOTA), ApeCoin (APE), and MultiversX (EGLD). Soft Staking enables users to earn staking rewards on selected tokens directly from their Spot Accounts, offering flexibility to trade, withdraw, or utilize tokens while still earning rewards. The rewards are generated through the on-chain proof of stake mechanism and are distributed daily in the respective native tokens to users' Spot Accounts. The calculation of rewards is based on the daily average balance of eligible tokens held in users' Spot Accounts.

With the inclusion of these new tokens, Binance's Soft Staking now supports a total of 15 tokens, including BNB, SOL, TRX, ADA, SUI, TON, NEAR, APT, POL, ALGO, IOTA, S, APE, EGLD, and AXS. To qualify for rewards, users must meet the minimum holding requirement for each token in their Spot balance, and there is a cap on the eligible holding amount. Balances exceeding this cap will not earn additional rewards. Users can activate Soft Staking via the Binance website or app by navigating to the Earn section, selecting Soft Staking, and agreeing to the terms. Average daily snapshots of the Spot Account begin at 00:00 (UTC) the day after activation, with rewards distributed the following day between 00:00 and 08:00 (UTC).

It is important to note that U.S. persons, entities, and individuals from sanctioned countries are restricted from using Binance Soft Staking. Users may also face access limitations due to regulatory constraints. Binance reserves the right to amend or cancel the announcement at any time without prior notice. The platform advises users to review the Binance Soft Staking Terms, Binance Terms of Use, and Risk Warning before participating.

data.title: Data: Monad's public offering has raised over $130 million, achieving 69.7% of its total goal.
data.description: According to a data dashboard shared by crypto KOL "Swishi.eth", the public sale of Monad (MON) tokens on the Coinbase platform has raised $130.7 million, achieving 69.7% of the total target, with an additional $22.9 million raised in the last 24 hours. This public sale offered 7.5% of the total MON tokens at a price of 0.025 USDC, with a FDV of $2.5 billion, and will close on November 23rd at 10:00 AM.

data.title: Jim Cramer: It feels like there's a cabal trying to keep Bitcoin above $90,000.
data.description: Jim Cramer posted on the X platform that it feels like there's a cabal trying to keep Bitcoin above $90,000. He said he likes Bitcoin but dislikes any derivatives created for trading, manipulation, or mining.

data.title: Bitwise CIO: Institutions value Bitcoin as a "service"
data.description: Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan countered market concerns about the recent decline. He believes Bitcoin's long-term value stems from its unique service of "digitally storing wealth without relying on any third party," rather than short-term price fluctuations. He likens Bitcoin to a service: just as the value of Microsoft stock depends on market demand for its services, Bitcoin's value is entirely determined by market demand for its "digital wealth storage" service. Increased demand leads to increased value; conversely, decreased demand leads to decreased value. The only way to access this service is to directly hold the asset. He points out that adoption by numerous institutional investors, from the Harvard endowment fund to sovereign wealth funds, demonstrates the authenticity and growth potential of its service demand. (The Block)

data.title: Blockchain News: Payment Firms Pick Polygon as Competition Intensifies
data.description:
In a recent Blockchain news update, Mastercard that it expanded its Crypto Credential identity service to self-custody wallets, selecting Polygon crypto as the first blockchain to support the rollout. The card network partnered with Mercuryo to introduce verified, username-based transfers. Users replace long wallet addresses with simple aliases while maintaining full control of their funds
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/11/19/blockchain-news-payment-firms-pick-polygon-as-competition-intensifies/

data.title: Bitget's cumulative trading volume for US stock contracts has surpassed $5 billion, with popular trading instruments including MSTR, TSLA, and AAPL.
data.description: Bitget's US stock futures trading volume has surpassed $5 billion. According to platform data, the top three most traded cryptocurrencies are MicroStrategy (MSTR), Tesla (TSLA), and Apple (AAPL), with cumulative trading volumes of $1.4 billion, $1 billion, and $472 million, respectively. Recently, Bitget launched a limited-time fee reduction promotion for stock futures. During the promotion period, users trading related stock futures pairs on the platform can enjoy a 90% reduction in fees. The promotion will last until January 31, 2026, at 23:59 (UTC+8).
